pullRequest.title: fix(cli): support configured tool defaults and interactive confirmations
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes tool loading in `ask` and `chat` commands, adds interactive confirmation mode for agents, and improves test coverage.

### Changes

- **`cli/_tool_names.py`** (new) — shared helper for parsing/normalising tool name lists
- **`cli/ask.py`** — fall back to `config.tools.enabled` when `--tools` flag is not provided; pass `interactive=True` and `confirm_callback` to agents
- **`cli/chat_cmd.py`** — same fallback logic; add y/N confirmation prompt before each tool call; fix regression for non-tool agents
- **`agents/_stubs.py`**, **`native_react.py`**, **`orchestrator.py`**, **`operative.py`**, **`rlm.py`**, **`monitor_operative.py`**, **`native_openhands.py`** — propagate `interactive` and `confirm_callback` parameters through the agent hierarchy
- **`tools/__init__.py`** — register `shell_exec` tool in auto-discovery
- **Tests** — add `test_ask_agent.py`, `test_chat_cmd.py`, extend `test_base_agent.py` and `test_shell_exec.py`

### Motivation

Closes #55 — when using `jarvis chat` or `jarvis ask` with an agent, tools listed in `config.toml` under `[tools] enabled` were silently ignored if the user didn't pass an explicit `--tools` flag. This made it impossible to use tools with ollama (or any local engine) without extra CLI flags every time.

### Testing

```
uv run pytest tests/cli/test_ask_agent.py tests/cli/test_chat_cmd.py tests/agents/test_base_agent.py tests/tools/test_shell_exec.py -v
uv run ruff check src/openjarvis/cli/ src/openjarvis/agents/
```

All checks pass.

pullRequest.title: fix: correct O(N²) energy/FLOPs savings calculation
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- **Fix O(N²) bug in energy savings calculation** (`src/openjarvis/server/savings.py`): The old code computed a `scale` factor (`flops / flops_with_cache`) that grew linearly with token count N, causing `energy_wh` to scale as O(N³) instead of O(N²). For Claude Opus with 5.8M tokens, this inflated energy by ~200,000x. Replaced with a direct `wh_per_flop` constant derived from each provider's existing `energy_wh_per_1k_tokens` and `flops_per_token` values.
- **Add 3 regression tests** (`tests/evals/test_use_case_benchmarks.py`): `test_energy_scales_linearly`, `test_energy_wh_matches_direct_formula`, `test_energy_not_zero` — all added to `TestSavings`.
- **Clarify comment in leaderboard JS** (`docs/javascripts/leaderboard.js`): Note that `ENERGY_WH_PER_FLOP` and the recompute functions are reference-only and unused by the leaderboard display.

Closes #95

## Test plan

- [x] `uv run pytest tests/evals/test_use_case_benchmarks.py::TestSavings -v` — 6/6 pass
- [x] `uv run pytest tests/evals/test_use_case_benchmarks.py -v` — 45/45 pass
- [x] `uv run ruff check` + `ruff format --check` — clean
- [x] Manual spot-check: `compute_savings(5_863_385, 0)` for claude-opus-4.6 produces `energy_wh ≈ 589M` (not trillions)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: fix: address installation failures and SDK bugs (#100)
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes all 4 bugs reported in #100:

- **Bug 1 — Rust toolchain not documented:** Added `maturin develop` build step to README, `docs/getting-started/installation.md` (Browser App, CLI, Python SDK sections), and `scripts/quickstart.sh`. Added Python 3.14 `PYO3_USE_ABI3_FORWARD_COMPATIBILITY` note.

- **Bug 2 — Namespace package conflict:** Moved `pyproject.toml` force-include targets from `openjarvis/` to `_node_modules/` so the Node.js modules no longer create a namespace package that shadows the real Python package in editable installs. Added fallback path resolution in `claude_code.py` and `whatsapp_baileys.py` for wheel installs.

- **Bug 3 — Trace Debugger always empty:** Enabled traces by default (`TracesConfig.enabled = True`). Fixed `api_routes.py` trace endpoint to use `store.list_traces()` (not the non-existent `.recent()`), `dataclasses.asdict()` (not the non-existent `.to_dict()`), and read the store from `app.state` instead of instantiating a new `TraceStore()` without arguments. Wired `TraceStore` into `app.state` in `app.py`.

- **Bug 4 — Thinking models return empty responses:** Removed hardcoded `"chat_template_kwargs": {"enable_thinking": False}` from `_openai_compat.py` that was suppressing thinking output for Qwen3, DeepSeek-R1, and other reasoning models on OpenAI-compatible engines (vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp). The streaming path already didn't set this flag.

## Test plan

- [x] `uv run ruff check` on all changed files — passes
- [x] 777 related tests pass (server, engine, learning)
- [x] No new test failures introduced
- [ ] Manual: `jarvis serve` → send query → Dashboard Trace Debugger shows traces
- [ ] Manual: thinking model via vLLM/SGLang returns non-empty content

Closes #100

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: fix: agent constructor defaults resolve from config.toml
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Agent constructors (`BaseAgent`, `ToolUsingAgent`, and all 8 concrete subclasses) had hardcoded defaults for `temperature`, `max_tokens`, and `max_turns` that silently ignored user settings in `~/.openjarvis/config.toml`
- Implemented 3-tier default resolution: **explicit arg > user config > class-level default > hardcoded fallback**
- Cached `load_config()` with `@lru_cache` to prevent repeated hardware detection (subprocess calls) on every agent instantiation

Closes #103

## What was broken

Setting `temperature`, `max_tokens`, or `max_turns` in `config.toml` had no effect when agents were constructed without explicit parameters (e.g., via `executor.py`, managed agent API, scheduler daemon). Each agent had its own hardcoded defaults baked into the constructor signature that took precedence over config.

## The fix

**`BaseAgent.__init__` and `ToolUsingAgent.__init__`** now accept `Optional[...] = None` for `temperature`, `max_tokens`, and `max_turns`. When `None`, they resolve from `load_config()`. On config failure, they fall back to class-level `_default_*` attributes (preserving agent-specific tuning).

**All 8 concrete agents** (`NativeReAct`, `NativeOpenHands`, `Orchestrator`, `MonitorOperative`, `Operative`, `RLM`, `OpenHands`, `ClaudeCode`) now declare their tuned defaults as class attributes:

| Agent | temperature | max_tokens | max_turns |
|-------|------------|------------|-----------|
| MonitorOperative | 0.3 | 4096 | 25 |
| Operative | 0.3 | 2048 | 20 |
| NativeOpenHands | 0.7 | 2048 | 3 |
| RLM | 0.7 | 2048 | 10 |
| Others | 0.7 | 1024 | 10 |

These class defaults are only used as a fallback when config is unavailable — user config always takes precedence.

## Test plan

- [x] 13 new tests in `tests/agents/test_config_defaults.py` covering config read, explicit override, partial override, config failure fallback, and class-level defaults
- [x] All 28 existing `test_base_agent.py` tests pass (backward compatible)
- [x] 392/392 agent tests pass (3 pre-existing failures on main unrelated to this change)
- [x] `ruff check` and `ruff format` clean
- [x] Original issue reproduced and confirmed fixed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: feat: remote engine host configuration via CLI
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #104.

Users with no local GPU/VRAM can now configure remote LLM engine endpoints (e.g., Ollama on another machine) directly from the CLI:

- **`jarvis config set engine.ollama.host http://<remote>:11434`** — write any config key from the command line, with key validation, type coercion, and engine host reachability probing
- **`jarvis init --engine ollama --host http://<remote>:11434`** — configure a remote host during first-time setup
- **Improved error messages** — when no engine is found, `jarvis ask` and `hint_no_engine()` now suggest remote configuration options including `jarvis config set` and `OLLAMA_HOST` env var

### Changes

- Added `tomlkit` dependency for comment-preserving TOML round-trip writes
- Added `validate_config_key()` helper that validates dotted keys (e.g. `engine.ollama.host`) against the `JarvisConfig` dataclass hierarchy
- Added `jarvis config set <key> <value>` subcommand with key validation, type coercion (str/int/float/bool), and engine host probing
- Added `--host` option to `jarvis init` that writes the remote URL into the generated config
- Updated `generate_minimal_toml()` to include a commented-out engine host hint
- Updated error messages in `ask.py` and `hints.py` to mention remote engine configuration

### Testing

- 35 new tests across 3 test files (+ 2 added to existing test file)
- All 69 relevant tests passing
- Manual E2E verification: config set → load_config → engine discovery → engine connects to configured host

## Test plan

- [ ] Run `uv run pytest tests/core/test_config_key_validation.py tests/cli/test_config_set.py tests/cli/test_init_host.py tests/cli/test_hints.py -v`
- [ ] Verify `jarvis config set engine.ollama.host http://<remote>:11434` writes config and probes host
- [ ] Verify `jarvis init --engine ollama --host http://<remote>:11434 --no-download` generates config with host
- [ ] Verify `jarvis ask "hello"` error message includes remote config hints when no engine is available

pullRequest.title: fix: correct Qwen3.5 model sizes and MLX repos in catalog
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Replace non-existent Qwen3.5 model sizes (3B, 8B, 14B) with actual sizes (0.8B, 2B, 9B, 27B)
- Fix `mlx_repo` URLs to point to real mlx-community repos on HuggingFace
- Update all references across CLI hints, tests, and eval configs

## Context

`jarvis init` on Apple Silicon with MLX engine recommends `qwen3.5:14b`, but that model doesn't exist — neither as a Qwen base model nor as an MLX community quantization. The catalog's `mlx_repo` values (`mlx-community/Qwen3.5-{3,8,14}B-4bit`) are all 404s.

Verified correct repos from https://huggingface.co/mlx-community:

| Model | MLX Repo |
|-------|----------|
| 0.8B | `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-0.8B-OptiQ-4bit` |
| 2B | `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-OptiQ-4bit` |
| 4B | `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-4B-OptiQ-4bit` |
| 9B | `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-9B-MLX-4bit` |
| 27B | `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-27B-4bit-DWQ` |

Fixes #129

- [x] `uv run ruff check` passes on changed files
- [x] `uv run ruff format --check` passes on changed files

pullRequest.title: feat: enhance security scan with DNS check, JSON output, and API endpoint
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Incorporates the useful new features proposed by @gridworks in #135 on top of the existing `PrivacyScanner` implementation (which already had 8 checks, platform filtering, `--quick` flag, and `jarvis init` integration).

**New features added:**
- DNS configuration check (macOS — detects plain vs encrypted DNS via `scutil --dns`)
- `--json` flag on `jarvis scan` for machine-readable output
- `--no-scan` flag on `jarvis init` to skip the post-init security audit
- Expanded remote-access process detection (ngrok, tailscaled, cloudflared, ZeroTier)
- Rich table output for `jarvis scan` (color-coded severity icons)
- `GET /v1/security/scan` API endpoint for web UI integration

**Preserves all existing functionality:**
- `PrivacyScanner` class and `ScanResult` dataclass unchanged
- All 8 existing checks retained (FileVault, MDM, iCloud, LUKS, cloud sync, network exposure, screen recording, remote access)
- `--quick` flag and `run_quick()` still work
- Platform-aware filtering still works

Closes #133

pullRequest.title: feat: add Codex cloud engine for ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscribers
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Adds `codex/` prefixed model support to the CloudEngine, allowing ChatGPT Plus and Pro subscribers to use OpenJarvis without a separate OpenAI API key.

Based on the [zeroclaw](https://github.com/zeroclaw-labs/zeroclaw) reference implementation, this uses the **OpenAI Responses API** at `chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses` — not the standard Chat Completions API.

**Key implementation details:**
- Auth via OAuth access token set in `OPENAI_CODEX_API_KEY` env var
- Uses Responses API format (`input` array, `instructions` for system prompt)
- Required header: `OpenAI-Beta: responses=experimental`
- Streaming via SSE with `response.output_text.delta` events
- Custom base URL supported via `OPENAI_CODEX_BASE_URL` env var
- `codex/` prefix stripped before sending model ID to API

**Models added:**
- `codex/gpt-4o`
- `codex/gpt-4o-mini`
- `codex/o3-mini`
- `codex/gpt-5-mini`
- `codex/gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07`

**Usage:**
```bash
export OPENAI_CODEX_API_KEY="your-chatgpt-oauth-token"
jarvis ask "Hello" --model codex/gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07
```

Closes #134

## Test plan

- [x] 11 new Codex tests (detection, client init, custom URL, list_models, generate with Responses API format, output block extraction, system→instructions mapping, close)
- [x] All 94 affected tests pass (cloud, cloud_extended, model_catalog_extended)
- [x] Ruff lint + format clean

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: chore(deps): bump mlx-lm to >=0.31.1 so Qwen3.5 architecture is suppo…
pullRequest.body:
…rted

uv.lock pinned mlx-lm==0.29.1, which predates the qwen3_5 architecture. Running mlx_lm.server with mlx-community/Qwen3.5-* models therefore fails with 'Model type qwen3_5 not supported.' on every uv sync, even when pyproject.toml's loose constraint (>=0.19) would otherwise allow a newer release.

Tighten the inference-mlx extra to mlx-lm>=0.31.1 (the first release with Qwen3.5 support) and regenerate uv.lock — resolves to mlx-lm 0.31.3.

Closes #191

pullRequest.title: fix(agents): bind built-in tools, live tool-call UI, Markdown streaming
pullRequest.body:
Closes #196 ## Summary Three related fixes to the Agents UI: 1. **Agents can now use their tools.** Template-declared tool strings like `"file_read"` were being passed straight to the engine, which expects OpenAI-shaped function specs — so nothing was bound to the model and agents hallucinated refusals (the "I only work in the OpenJarvis repo" reply). Added a resolver that looks each name up in `ToolRegistry` and wraps its `.spec` as `{type: "function", function: {...}}`. Ollama's streaming path was also silently dropping the `tools` kwarg; added `OllamaEngine.stream_full()` that forwards tools to `/api/chat`, parses `tool_calls` from streamed chunks, and falls back to a tools-less retry on 400. 2. **Tool calls render as their own messages in the chat flow** (live + persisted across reload). Unified SSE event names to `tool_call_start` / `tool_call_end` to match the main chat (`stream_bridge.py`), emitted from both the regular and deep-research paths with full args, result, latency. Added a `tool_calls` TEXT column on `agent_messages` so the UI can re-render them after a page refresh. Rewrote `sendAgentMessage`'s SSE parser to track `event:` lines and expose `onToolCallStart` / `onToolCallEnd` callbacks. 3. **Markdown streams progressively** (including tables). The streaming bubble was a plain `

` and only converted to Markdown after the stream ended; now it uses `` like the main chat. Also: compact ToolCallCard (monospace, inline args preview, pretty-printed JSON on expand), tool-call count + per-call args in the expanded response footer, scroll-to-bottom on send. ## Test plan - [x] `uv run --extra dev --extra server pytest tests/agents/test_manager.py tests/server/test_agent_manager_routes.py::TestResolveToolSpecs tests/engine/test_ollama.py` — 43 tests green - [x] `cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit && npx vite build` — clean - [x] Manual: create an agent from the web UI, send a message that should trigger a tool (e.g. "read ~/Downloads"), verify the tool-call card appears live with name + args, then the agent's reply streams beneath it with Markdown formatting intact (tables, headings) - [x] Manual: reload the page after the agent replies — tool-call cards persist in the transcript - [x] Manual: expand the response footer — shows `1 tool call` with the tool name and arguments

pullRequest.title: fix(server): replace CORS wildcard fallback with closed localhost list
pullRequest.body:
server/app.py defaulted to allow_origins=["*"] combined with allow_credentials=True when cors_origins was not passed. That combination is invalid per the CORS spec — browsers reject it — and when used loosely it signals that any cross-origin request with credentials is allowed, exposing session cookies and auth headers.

The reference config at configs/openjarvis/config.toml binds to 0.0.0.0 without setting cors_origins, so this fallback fired in every default deployment.

Default to a closed list (Vite dev origin + Tauri webview) instead of the wildcard. Users who actually need a custom origin already pass it via cors_origins.

Closes #222

pullRequest.title: test: add live marker, CI coverage gate, rust bridge boundary tests
pullRequest.body:
Closes #223.

## Summary
- Mark `test_live_obsidian_full_pipeline` with `@pytest.mark.live` so the CI filter `-m "not live and not cloud"` deselects it.
- Run pytest under coverage in CI: `--cov=openjarvis`, term-missing + xml reports, `--cov-fail-under=60` (current baseline measured at ~63%), and upload `coverage.xml` as an artifact.
- Add 12 boundary tests for the rust bridge JSON converters (`scan_result_from_json`, `injection_result_from_json`, `retrieval_results_from_json`) covering malformed JSON, missing keys, unknown threat levels, metadata decode failure, score coercion, and non-list top-level inputs.

## Notes / deviations from issue
- Issue suggested `--cov=src/openjarvis`; coverage takes module names not paths, so this PR uses `--cov=openjarvis`.
- Skipped `codecov/codecov-action@v4` because it would need a `CODECOV_TOKEN` secret to be configured — artifact upload gives visibility without new repo config. Easy to add later.
- Out of scope but worth flagging: `tests/server/test_agent_manager_routes.py` and `tests/server/test_connectors_router.py` have ~13 tests already failing on `main` due to stale fixtures. Those need a separate fix before the new coverage gate is useful in CI.

## Test plan
- [x] `pytest tests/core/test_rust_bridge.py` — 23 passed (11 existing + 12 new)
- [x] `pytest tests/connectors/test_live_smoke.py -m "not live and not cloud" --collect-only` — 1 deselected (confirms marker works)
- [x] Full suite with coverage locally — 5832 passed, baseline coverage 63%
- [ ] CI run against this PR

pullRequest.title: fix(config): drop [security] enabled=false from reference config so s…
pullRequest.body:
…ecurity middleware ships on by default

configs/openjarvis/config.toml is the file users copy as their starting configuration. It explicitly set [security] enabled = false (with a comment about eval performance) — meaning users who follow the quickstart unknowingly run with GuardrailsEngine, InjectionScanner, CapabilityPolicy, and rate limiting all disabled.

The SecurityConfig.enabled default in code is True, so removing the override here lets every install ship with security on. Eval workflows that genuinely need it disabled can opt out via their own config.

Closes #224

pullRequest.title: refactor(system): decompose JarvisSystem god-object into system/ package
pullRequest.body:
Closes #226.

`src/openjarvis/system.py` was 1289 lines with `JarvisSystem` as an `Optional[Any]`-typed god-object holding ~25 subsystems plus ~400 lines of query-execution behavior, plus an ~800-line `SystemBuilder`. This PR breaks it up incrementally.

## Summary

- **Typed fields** — 25 `Optional[Any]` → real types via `TYPE_CHECKING` imports.
- **Bundles** — `SecurityContext` / `Observability` / `AgentRuntime` / `Scheduling` dataclasses; `system.security`, `system.agents`, etc.
- **Extracted `QueryOrchestrator`** — moved `ask()` / `_run_agent()` (~400 lines) out of `JarvisSystem`. `JarvisSystem.ask()` is a one-line delegate.
- **`OrchestratorDeps` Protocol** — lets tests pass a minimal fake instead of a full `JarvisSystem`. 10 isolated unit tests added.
- **`system/` package** — moved the four new modules into `src/openjarvis/system/`. `__init__.py` re-exports everything, so existing `from openjarvis.system import JarvisSystem` callers keep working.
- **Registry-ized `_resolve_channel`** — the 115-line `if key == "telegram" / elif ...` switch becomes a dict of per-channel kwargs mappers in `_channel_kwargs.py`.
- **Extracted `SystemBuilder`** to `builder.py`; `core.py` is now just the `JarvisSystem` dataclass.

## Final package layout

| File | Lines | Contents |
|---|---:|---|
| `system/__init__.py` | 23 | re-exports |
| `system/core.py` | 320 | `JarvisSystem` dataclass |
| `system/builder.py` | 621 | `SystemBuilder` |
| `system/orchestrator.py` | 314 | `QueryOrchestrator` (behavior) |
| `system/bundles.py` | 59 | bounded-context dataclasses |
| `system/protocols.py` | 40 | `OrchestratorDeps` |
| `system/_channel_kwargs.py` | 199 | channel kwargs registry |

## Test plan

- [x] `from openjarvis.system import JarvisSystem, SystemBuilder` still works (all 34 existing callers)
- [x] Full pytest on this branch vs `main`: 145 failures, `diff` shows IDENTICAL — zero regressions
- [x] +10 new isolated `QueryOrchestrator` tests in `tests/test_query_orchestrator.py` pass
- [x] Two patch targets in tests updated: `openjarvis.system.core.logger` → `openjarvis.system.builder.logger`; `JarvisSystem._run_agent` → `QueryOrchestrator._run_agent`
- [x] `ruff` / `ruff-format` pre-commit hooks pass

## Commits

1. Replace `Optional[Any]` with real types
2. Add bounded-context bundle snapshots
3. Extract `QueryOrchestrator`
4. Add `OrchestratorDeps` Protocol + isolated tests
5. Package up `system/`
6. Registry-ize channel kwargs
7. Extract `SystemBuilder` to `system/builder.py`

pullRequest.title: fix(server): allow 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' in CSP so dashboard …
pullRequest.body:
…renders after Rust module compiles

The dashboard at /dashboard renders blank once the Rust security module is compiled (uv run maturin develop ...). The middleware sets "Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'", which blocks the inline scripts and styles the dashboard HTML relies on.

Loosen the policy to allow 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' so the dashboard works once the security middleware is active. The same string is exported via SECURITY_HEADERS for tests, so update both call sites.

Closes #261

pullRequest.title: fix(memory): default context_min_score to 0.0 so FTS5 BM25 results ar…
pullRequest.body:
…en't dropped on small corpora

SQLite FTS5 BM25 scores collapse toward zero on small corpora — when every indexed document contains the query term, IDF approaches zero so scores return ~1e-6. The default context_min_score = 0.1 silently filters out every result before injection, so memory is stored but never reaches the model on a fresh install.

Lowering both defaults to 0.0 lets retrieved context flow through; users who want strict filtering can still set [memory] context_min_score in config.toml.

Closes #262

pullRequest.title: docs: align quickstart model with CPU-friendly recommendation (qwen3.…
pullRequest.body:
…5:4b)

Reported via twitter (issue #269): the macOS install guide and other docs recommend a 4B model on CPU but the README and quickstart pull / ask a much larger model (qwen3:8b) — confusing and slow for first-time CPU-only users.

Switch the README quickstart and the docs/getting-started/quickstart.md 'Chat with Any Model' tile to qwen3.5:4b, with an inline note that GPU users can scale up to qwen3.5:9b or larger. This matches the existing chat-simple preset and the macOS guide's CPU recommendation.

Closes #269
